title: 7.-Contract No: 32300104 Amendment: The Village of Love for Telegraph Neighborhood Sacred Rest Drop-In Center

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or designee, to execute an
amendment to Contract No. 32300104 with The Village of Love to add $250,000, for a
total contract amount not to exceed $1,000,000, for services and operations at the
Telegraph Neighborhood Sacred Rest Drop-In Center and extend the contract term
through May 31, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
This amendment will be funded using (Fund 147). Funds are budgeted in the Fiscal
Year 2026 (FY2026) budget and are subject to the adoption of the FY2026 budget and
the FY2026 Annual Appropriations Ordinance.
sections.current_situation:
The Village of Love currently operates the Telegraph Neighborhood Sacred Rest Drop-
In Center (Center), a daytime drop-in center for people experiencing homelessness.
The Center began operating in June 2022 and has helped over 300 unhoused
community members get connected to services and housing readiness resources in the
community. The Center is open Monday through Friday from 9:30 am to 5:30 pm and
offers access to restrooms and basic services, including respite, meals, and housing
navigation.
The existing contract is expected to expire on May 31, 2025, unless it is amended to
include additional funding. UC Berkeley has allocated $250,000 to extend the
contracted services through May 31, 2026. The Village of Love needs an additional
$250,000 to maintain its current operations. The Center is supported by the University’s
Homeless Outreach Coordinator, as well as students from UC Berkeley. It is an
important component of the City and University’s joint effort to resolve homelessness in
Berkeley, by tailoring services to each individual’s needs and wants. The Village of Love
holds the lease with First Presbyterian Church for access to and use of the site.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Contract Amendment: Amendment No: 32300104 with The Village of Love CONSENT CALENDAR

sections.background:
On July 27, 2021, the City and University executed the Joint Settlement Agreement,
which, among other things, committed the City to expend an amount determined
through joint planning between the City and the University to “fund a day-time drop-
in/service center in the Telegraph area for the unhoused population.” On January 18,
2022, the leadership of the First Presbyterian Church of Berkeley unanimously
approved providing access to a vacant lot on Haste Street owned by the Church for the
drop-in center. On March 2, 2022, the University awarded a separate $500,000 contract
to The Village of Love to provide services and operations at the drop-in center at this
site for two years, through May 2024. In May 2024, the University added $250,000 to
extend services through May 31, 2025. On May 21, 2024 Council adopted Resolution
No. 71,344-N.S. to allocate $250,000 to extend services through May 31, 2025.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental impacts associated with this report.
sections.rationale:
There continues to be a need to provide basic services and support to unhoused
residents who are unhoused in the greater Telegraph District/Southside neighborhood.
The agreement between the University and the City of Berkeley to continue to fund a
daytime drop-in center in the Telegraph area for the unhoused population targets helps
address the unmet need for services in the neighborhood.
sections.alternatives:
None. Since the University already identified a service provider, a leasing mechanism
for the site, paid the full cost of the capital expenses associated with the development of
the drop-in center, and continues to provide a 50% match for operations.
CONTACT PERSON
David Andujo-Walker, Community Services Specialist II, 510-981-5454
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32300104 AMENDMENT WITH THE VILLAGE OF LOVE
WHEREAS, People’s Park was home to an encampment of unsheltered individuals who
need an array of services to end their unsheltered status; and
WHEREAS, in July 2021, the City and University of California, Berkeley entered into the
UC Berkeley – City of Berkeley Joint Settlement Agreement; and
WHEREAS, the first annual payment of the Settlement Agreement requires that an
amount determined through joint planning between the City and the University be
allocated to fund a day-time drop-in/service center in the Telegraph area for the unhoused
population; and
WHEREAS, on January 18, 2022, Leadership at the First Presbyterian Church of
Berkeley voted unanimously to grant access to and use of a vacant lot on Haste Street
owned by the Church to the University for purposes of establishing a daytime drop-in
center; and
WHEREAS, the University awarded a $500,000 grant on March 2, 2022 to The Village of
Love to provide services and operations for the drop-in center at this site for two years
through May 31, 2024; and
WHEREAS, the Berkeley City Council allocated $250,000 on April 26, 2022 and on May
23, 2023 for services and operations at the drop-in center for one year through May 31,
2023 and May 31, 2024 respectively; and
WHEREAS, the University awarded an additional $250,000 grant to The Village of Love
to continue services and operations through May 31, 2025; and
WHEREAS, the Berkeley City Council allocated $250,000 on May 21, 2024 to continue
services and operations at the drop-in center for one year through May 31, 2025; and
WHEREAS, funds in the amount of $250,000 are needed to continue services and
operations through May 31, 2026.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes
the City Manager or designee to execute an amendment to add $250,000 using Fund 147
for a total contract amount not to exceed $1,000,000 and extend

This amendment will be funded using (Fund 147). Funds are budgeted in the Fiscal

the FY2026 Annual Appropriations Ordinance.
